items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Let users define named profiles like 'work', 'oss', 'client-acme' that include different target sets, section filters, and MCP server subsets. One command switches the active profile. Solves the problem of developers who use different tool stacks for different contexts and don't want everything synced everywhere.

items.title: Team Config Broadcast via Git
items.description: Sync a shared team CLAUDE.md from a git remote (e.g., company dotfiles repo) before distributing to each team member's local harnesses. HarnessSync acts as the bridge between team-level canonical config and individual developer harness setups. Eliminates 'works on my machine' when a teammate added a rule nobody else has.

items.title: Drift Alerts via Native OS Notifications
items.description: When a target harness config file is manually edited outside of HarnessSync (detected via file hash), fire a macOS/Linux desktop notification with a one-click re-sync option. Solves config drift that happens silently when users tweak Cursor rules directly and forget to propagate the change back.

items.title: Live Capability Matrix with Gap Warnings
items.description: After sync, show a table of which capabilities from Claude Code actually mapped successfully vs. which had no equivalent in each target. Include specific examples: 'Your /refactor skill has no Codex equivalent—users on Codex won't have this.' Helps users understand what they're giving up when colleagues use a different harness.

items.title: MCP Server Reachability Dashboard
items.description: Show a real-time health panel for every MCP server across all synced harnesses—whether each endpoint is reachable, latency, last successful call. Solves the mystery of 'why does my agent work in Claude Code but fail in OpenCode?' by surfacing MCP connectivity issues per harness.

items.title: Auto-Discovery of Installed Harnesses
items.description: On first run or /sync-setup, scan the system for installed harnesses (check PATH, brew, npm, pip, common install locations) and auto-configure sync targets without manual setup. New harnesses added later trigger a notification. Removes the friction of manually telling HarnessSync what tools you have.

items.title: Section-Level Sync Control per Target
items.description: Let users mark specific sections of CLAUDE.md with tags like `<!-- harness:codex-only -->` or `<!-- harness:skip-gemini -->` so sections sync only to appropriate targets. Solves the need to have Codex-specific instructions that shouldn't pollute Gemini's context.

items.title: Config Snapshots with Tags
items.description: Let users tag the current synced state with a name ('before-big-refactor', 'v2.1-release-setup') and restore to any named snapshot later. Goes beyond the existing rollback to give meaningful named checkpoints. Solves the 'I changed my rules 2 weeks ago and things got worse but I don't know what I changed' problem.

items.title: 3-Way Merge for Config Conflicts
items.description: When a target file has been manually edited AND Claude Code config has changed, present a structured diff showing base, Claude Code version, and manual edits—then ask the user which changes to keep. Prevents the current silent overwrite that destroys deliberate target-specific customizations.

items.title: Harness Onboarding Wizard for New Team Members
items.description: A guided /sync-onboard command that walks a new developer through installing supported harnesses, authenticating accounts, selecting sync targets, and running the first sync—all in one interactive flow. Reduces the 'I joined a team that uses HarnessSync, how do I get started?' friction from hours to minutes.

items.title: CI/CD Config Validation Action
items.description: A GitHub Actions / GitLab CI step that runs HarnessSync in lint-only mode on PRs, failing the build if CLAUDE.md changes would produce invalid or unsyncable output for any target. Catches config regressions before they reach teammates' machines.

items.title: Plugin & Skill Marketplace Discovery
items.description: A /sync-discover command that scans a curated registry for Claude Code plugins and shows which ones have equivalents for each target harness. When you install a new plugin, HarnessSync automatically sets up the best available approximation for Codex, Gemini, etc. Grows the value of the plugin ecosystem for multi-harness users.

items.title: Cross-Harness Env Var & Secret Management
items.description: Sync environment variable declarations and tool permission allow-lists across harnesses in a format-appropriate way (Claude Code settings.json → Codex config.toml → Gemini settings.json). Flag secrets that appear in plaintext and suggest moving to a keychain reference. Solves the mess of re-declaring API keys in 5 different config files.

items.title: Local Sync Analytics & Usage Insights
items.description: Track which rules and skills are actually being triggered across harnesses (via PostToolUse hooks) and surface a weekly digest: 'Your /refactor skill was used 47 times in Claude Code but never in Codex—consider whether Codex users know it exists.' Helps users understand which parts of their config actually deliver value.

items.title: Harness-Specific Override Files
items.description: Support a `CLAUDE.codex.md` / `CLAUDE.gemini.md` pattern where target-specific additions are merged on top of the base CLAUDE.md during sync to that target. Solves the need to add Codex-specific caveats (e.g., 'Note: Codex cannot run shell commands') without polluting the canonical Claude Code config.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Parity Tester
items.description: Run the same prompt/task against multiple synced harnesses and compare their responses side-by-side to verify that synced rules are actually producing equivalent behavior. Answers 'did my sync actually make Gemini behave like Claude Code?' with real evidence instead of config inspection.

items.title: AI-Assisted Rule Translation for Gaps
items.description: When a Claude Code rule or skill has no direct equivalent in a target harness, use Claude to generate the best approximation in the target's native format rather than silently skipping it. Show a confidence score and let users accept/reject. Closes the biggest gap in current sync: unmappable features just disappear.

items.title: Scheduled Automatic Sync
items.description: Allow users to configure HarnessSync to run a full sync on a schedule (hourly, daily, on login) via launchd/systemd, not just on file change. Useful for users who edit CLAUDE.md externally (in another editor) and want eventual consistency without manual triggering.

items.title: Permission Model Auditor
items.description: Compare Claude Code's allowed/denied tool permissions against what each target harness exposes and flag mismatches—e.g., 'You've blocked shell access in Claude Code but Codex has no equivalent restriction.' Solves the security gap where a locked-down Claude Code config gives false confidence that other harnesses are equally restricted.

items.title: Auto-Generated Sync Changelog
items.description: After each sync, append a human-readable entry to a SYNC_CHANGELOG.md describing what changed in plain language ('Added 3 new rules from CLAUDE.md, removed deprecated /old-skill, updated MCP server URL for context7'). Gives teams an audit trail of how their AI config evolved over time.

items.title: Multi-Project Sync Hub View
items.description: A dashboard showing sync status across ALL projects on the machine (not just the current one), with last-sync time, drift score, and quick-sync buttons per project. Solves the problem of forgetting to sync a project you haven't worked on in a week and showing up in Cursor with stale rules.

items.title: Harness Config Deprecation Warnings
items.description: Monitor upstream harness release notes (Codex, Gemini, OpenCode changelogs) and warn when HarnessSync is writing to a config key that has been deprecated or removed in a recent harness version. Prevents silent breakage when harnesses update their config schemas.

items.title: Shareable Config Export for Teammates
items.description: A /sync-export command that bundles the canonical Claude Code config into a portable tarball or gist that a teammate can bootstrap from with one command on their machine. Solves the 'here's how I have my AI set up' sharing problem for teams adopting HarnessSync together.

items.title: Rule Source Attribution in Synced Files
items.description: Embed comments in synced output files (where the format supports it) noting which section came from which source file and when it was last synced. Makes it easy for teammates or future-you to understand why a Codex AGENTS.md rule exists and where to go to change it.

items.title: Harness Version Update Detector
items.description: Detect when a supported harness (Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) has been updated to a new version and re-run compatibility checks to see if any sync mappings need to be updated. Notify the user with a /sync-status flag. Prevents the silent failure mode where a harness update breaks sync without anyone noticing.

items.title: Project-Type Detection with Rule Suggestions
items.description: Analyze the current project (language, framework, test runner, CI system) and suggest CLAUDE.md rules tailored to that stack, formatted for all synced harnesses. E.g., a Next.js project gets suggested rules about TypeScript, ESLint, and Vercel deployment. Reduces the blank-page problem when setting up AI config for a new project.

items.title: Symlink Health Monitor
items.description: Continuously verify that all symlinks created by HarnessSync (for skills/agents) are still valid—pointing to existing source files and not broken by moves or renames. Surface broken symlinks in /sync-health and offer auto-repair. Prevents the invisible failure where a skill exists in Claude Code but silently 404s in Codex.

items.title: Config Complexity Benchmark
items.description: Score your Claude Code config on a set of dimensions (rule specificity, skill coverage, MCP breadth, security posture) and compare it to community averages or team norms. A 'config health score' that gamifies improving your AI setup. Gives users who aren't sure what to add next a concrete direction.

items.title: Pre-Sync Validation Hooks
items.description: Allow users to define shell scripts or Python validators that run before each sync and can block it if they fail (e.g., 'don't sync if CLAUDE.md contains TODO', 'require all MCPs to be reachable first'). Makes HarnessSync fit into team workflows that have guardrails around config changes.

items.title: VS Code Extension Config Bridge
items.description: Sync Claude Code rules and MCP servers into VS Code AI extension configs (Continue, Copilot Instructions, Cline) in addition to CLI harnesses. Bridges the CLI-vs-IDE gap for users who switch between terminal and VS Code. Dramatically expands the total addressable audience beyond pure CLI users.

items.title: PR Sync Impact Comment
items.description: When a PR modifies CLAUDE.md or any Claude Code config, automatically post a PR comment summarizing how those changes will affect each synced harness (what maps, what doesn't, any deprecated keys). Helps reviewers understand the downstream impact of config changes without needing to run HarnessSync locally.

items.title: Global Hotkey Sync Trigger
items.description: Register a system-wide keyboard shortcut (configurable) that triggers an immediate full sync from anywhere on the desktop, without needing to be in a terminal. For power users who edit CLAUDE.md in their editor and want instant propagation without context-switching to a terminal.

items.title: Gradual Harness Rollout
items.description: When rolling out a significant rule or skill change, sync to one harness first, let the user validate, then propagate to the rest in stages. Useful for teams who want to test a new strict rule in Gemini before pushing it to everyone's Codex setup. Reduces the blast radius of config experiments.

items.title: Natural Language Rule Authoring
items.description: A /sync-author command where users describe in plain English what behavior they want ('always suggest tests when writing new functions') and Claude generates the appropriate CLAUDE.md rule AND the best approximation for each target harness simultaneously. Lowers the bar for users who know what they want but not how to write it.

items.title: Offline Sync Queue
items.description: Queue sync operations that couldn't complete (e.g., a remote MCP target was unreachable, or a network drive was offline) and automatically replay them when connectivity is restored. Prevents lost syncs from failing silently when working in disconnected environments.

items.title: Config Bloat Detector
items.description: Analyze CLAUDE.md and synced configs for redundant rules, conflicting instructions, and dead skills (defined but never referenced) and suggest cleanup. Shows how many tokens of context each rule consumes across harnesses. Helps users whose configs have grown organically and are now hurting LLM performance due to context bloat.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.
